description_long: This object is a vintage-style CRT television with a bulky, deep chassis and a slightly curved front display. The casing is predominantly matte black with visible scuffs and wear, suggesting age and frequent use. The front features a large, glass screen bordered by a thick plastic bezel, with several control buttons and a small logo or label beneath the display. The back of the television is heavily vented with horizontal slats for heat dissipation, and a recessed area likely for cable connections. The overall form is trapezoidal, with the rear section much deeper than the front, characteristic of older cathode ray tube designs. The television appears robust and heavy, suitable for placement on a sturdy surface in an urban environment.
description: A bulky, black CRT television with a curved glass screen, thick bezel, and vented back panel, showing signs of wear.
description_view_0: The left rear side of the television, showing the deep, slanted back and vented upper section.
description_view_1: The front view, highlighting the large curved screen, thick bezel, and control buttons below the display.
description_view_2: The right rear side, mirroring the left with visible scuffs and the slanted, deep chassis.
description_view_3: The back view, dominated by large horizontal vents and a recessed area likely for ports or cable connections.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, matte black plastic bezel with rounded corners and a slightly recessed screen.
attribute_control_button_layout: cluster of buttons on the bottom left edge of the front panel
